records.ety: [OE. schethe, AS. scǣð, sceð, scēð; akin to OS. skēðia, D. scheede, G. scheide, OHG. sceida, Sw. skida, Dan. skede, Icel. skeiðir, pl., and to E. shed, v.t., originally meaning, to separate, to part. See Shed.]

defs: A case for the reception of a sword, hunting knife, or other long and slender instrument; a scabbard.
quotes: The dead knight's sword out of his sheath he drew.

defs: Any sheathlike covering, organ, or part.
The base of a leaf when sheathing or investing a stem or branch, as in grasses.
One of the elytra of an insect.
